senses.definition: A small brine shrimp that lives in very salty lakes and is often used as food for fish in aquariums.
examples: The scientist collected artemia from the salt lake for her experiment.
Many aquarium owners feed artemia to their baby fish.
Under the microscope, the artemia swam quickly through the water.

senses.definition: In ancient Greek mythology, the goddess of hunting, wild animals, and the moon.
examples: In the myth, Artemis protects the forest and its animals.
The temple was built in honor of Artemis.
Artemis is often shown carrying a bow and arrows.

senses.definition: The name of a United States space program that plans missions to the Moon and beyond.
examples: NASA’s Artemis program aims to return humans to the Moon.
The new rocket is being tested for the Artemis missions.
Many countries are joining the Artemis project.

senses.definition: A plant of the wormwood family, including herbs and shrubs that often have a strong smell and are used in medicine or as flavoring.
examples: The herbal tea contained artemisia leaves.
Farmers sometimes use artemisia to keep insects away from crops.
Traditional healers have long valued artemisia for its bitter taste.

senses.definition: Plural of artemisia: several plants of the wormwood family, often used as herbs or in traditional medicine.
examples: Different artemisias grow in dry, sunny places.
The garden includes several artemisias for their silver leaves.
Researchers compared the chemical oils in various artemisias.

senses.definition: Relating to or derived from plants of the genus Artemisia.
examples: The oil contains several artemisic compounds.
Artemisic acids were isolated from the plant extract.
The researchers measured the artemisic content of the sample.

senses.definition: A chemical compound related to artemisinin, obtained from Artemisia plants, studied for medical use.
examples: The laboratory is testing artemisin for its antimalarial effects.
Artemisin derivatives were compared with other drugs.
They analyzed artemisin levels in the extract.

senses.definition: A drug taken from the sweet wormwood plant and used to treat malaria.
examples: The doctor prescribed an artemisinin combination therapy.
Artemisinin has greatly reduced deaths from severe malaria.
Researchers are studying how artemisinin kills malaria parasites.

senses.definition: Several forms of artemisinin and its related drugs used to treat malaria.
examples: Modern malaria treatment often uses artemisinins.
The study compared different artemisinins for safety and effect.
Resistance to artemisinins is a serious global concern.

senses.definition: An ancient Greek temple dedicated to the goddess Artemis.
examples: The ruins of the artemision attracted many historians.
Archaeologists uncovered new columns from the old artemision.
The artemision once served as a major religious center.

senses.definition: Another name used in historical texts for a temple of Artemis or a place related to her worship.
examples: Coins from the region show the famous artemisium.
Pilgrims once traveled long distances to the artemisium.
The artemisium played an important role in local festivals.

senses.definition: A type of small sail on an ancient ship, usually set on the bow.
examples: The crew raised the artemon as the wind changed.
A damaged artemon slowed the old vessel.
Sailors argued about the best angle for the artemon.

senses.definition: Non-standard or dialect spelling of "artery".
examples: In the old text, the word "arter" was used instead of "artery".
Some dialect writers used "arter" to show local speech.
The doctor corrected the term "arter" to the standard "artery".

senses.definition: A technical or Latin-based word for an artery, a blood vessel that carries blood away from the heart.
examples: The diagram labelled the main arteria in the leg.
Damage to an arteria can cause serious bleeding.
Doctors examined the patient’s coronary arteria during surgery.

senses.definition: Relating to or affecting an artery.
examples: Arteriac damage was visible in the scan.
The report described arteriac changes in the tissue.
Arteriac disease often develops slowly.

senses.definition: Plural of arteria, used in medical Latin to refer to several arteries.
examples: The diagram showed the main arteriae of the neck.
The surgeon tied off several small arteriae during the operation.
In the textbook, the cranial arteriae are listed in detail.

senses.definition: Severe pain believed to come from an artery or arterial disease.
examples: The patient reported sudden arteriagra in the chest.
Doctors tried to find the cause of the arteriagra.
Arteriagra can be a sign of blocked blood flow.

senses.definition: Relating to an artery, the blood vessels that carry blood from the heart around the body.
examples: The doctor measured the patient’s arterial blood pressure.
Smoking increases the risk of arterial disease.
The injury caused serious damage to an arterial wall.

senses.definition: Describing a main route, road, or channel where a lot of movement or traffic happens.
examples: The city plans to widen the main arterial road.
This bridge connects two important arterial routes.
Heavy trucks use the arterial highway to cross the region.

senses.definition: The process of making blood more like arterial blood by adding oxygen, or making a vessel more like an artery.
examples: The lungs are responsible for the arterialisation of venous blood.
Arterialisation occurs as blood passes through the lungs.
The surgeon aimed for arterialisation of the grafted vein.

senses.definition: To make blood or a vessel more like an artery, especially by increasing its oxygen content.
examples: The lungs arterialise the blood before it returns to the heart.
This machine arterialises venous blood outside the body.
The procedure arterialises the transplanted vein.

senses.definition: Describing blood or a vessel that has been made like an artery, usually by gaining oxygen.
examples: The sample contained arterialised blood from the patient’s hand.
Arterialised veins can withstand higher pressure.
They studied the properties of arterialised tissue.

senses.definition: Third person singular of arterialise: makes blood or a vessel more like an artery, especially by adding oxygen.
examples: The membrane arterialises the blood as it passes through.
This device arterialises venous blood very quickly.
In the body, lung tissue arterialises incoming blood.

senses.definition: Present participle of arterialise: making blood or a vessel more like an artery, especially more oxygen-rich.
examples: The machine is arterialising the blood outside the body.
Arterialising the vein helps it carry more blood.
They monitored the process of arterialising the sample.

senses.definition: The process of making blood or tissue more like that in an artery, especially by adding oxygen.
examples: Arterialization of venous blood happens in the lungs.
The device allows rapid arterialization during surgery.
Doctors monitored the arterialization of the graft.

senses.definition: To make blood or a vessel more like an artery, especially by increasing its oxygen content.
examples: The lungs arterialize blood returning from the body.
This equipment arterializes the patient’s blood during bypass surgery.
Surgeons hope to arterialize the transplanted vein.

senses.definition: Describing blood or tissue that has been made like arterial blood, usually by adding oxygen.
examples: The sample of arterialized blood was taken from the wrist.
Arterialized veins can serve as new arteries.
They compared arterialized tissue with normal tissue.

senses.definition: Third person singular of arterialize: makes blood or a vessel more like an artery, especially more oxygen-rich.
examples: The membrane arterializes the blood flowing through it.
This machine arterializes venous blood before it returns to the body.
Lung tissue arterializes blood during each breath.

senses.definition: Present participle of arterialize: making blood or a vessel more like arterial blood by adding oxygen.
examples: The device is arterializing the patient’s blood.
Arterializing the vein helps it function as an artery.
They observed the effects of arterializing the sample.

senses.definition: In a way that relates to arteries or arterial blood.
examples: The medication is delivered arterially rather than through a vein.
The tissue is supplied arterially from the main trunk.
Blood flows arterially from the heart to the organs.

senses.definition: Plural of arterial; main roads or routes that carry a lot of traffic.
examples: Traffic was heavy on the city’s main arterials.
The plan includes new bike lanes along key arterials.
Snowplows first clear the arterials and then the side streets.

senses.definition: Abnormal narrowing or constriction of an artery.
examples: Severe arteriarctia can reduce blood flow to the heart.
The scan confirmed arteriarctia in the leg artery.
Smoking is a risk factor for arteriarctia.

senses.definition: Inflammation or disease of an artery.
examples: The biopsy suggested arteriasis in the affected vessel.
Untreated arteriasis can damage the arterial wall.
Doctors are studying rare forms of arteriasis.

senses.definition: Abnormal widening or dilation of an artery.
examples: Arteriectasia can lead to the formation of an aneurysm.
The scan revealed arteriectasia of the abdominal aorta.
Doctors monitored the patient’s arteriectasia over time.

senses.definition: Abnormal stretching or expansion of an artery.
examples: The ultrasound showed signs of arteriectasis.
Arteriectasis may weaken the arterial wall.
Long-term high blood pressure can cause arteriectasis.

senses.definition: Surgical removal of a part of an artery.
examples: The patient underwent an arteriectomy to remove the damaged section.
Arteriectomy may restore blood flow in severe disease.
The surgeon discussed the risks of arteriectomy.

senses.definition: Abnormal position or displacement of an artery.
examples: Imaging showed arteriectopia of the renal artery.
Arteriectopia can complicate surgical planning.
The doctor explained how arteriectopia affected blood flow.

senses.definition: Having arteries, especially in a description of tissue or an organ.
examples: The arteried tissue was carefully examined.
An arteried flap was used in the reconstruction.
The sample showed well-arteried muscle.

senses.definition: Plural of artery; tubes that carry blood away from the heart to different parts of the body.
examples: Healthy arteries are important for a strong heart.
Fat can build up in the arteries and block blood flow.
The doctor checked the arteries in his neck.

senses.definition: Main routes or channels, especially major roads or paths used for moving people or goods.
examples: The city’s main arteries are crowded during rush hour.
New rail arteries connect the port to the capital.
Economic growth depends on strong transport arteries.

senses.definition: A chemical substance related to arterial tissue or function, mentioned in specialized medical literature.
examples: Researchers isolated arterin from the tissue sample.
Arterin levels were higher in patients with the disease.
The role of arterin in blood pressure control is not clear.

senses.definition: Narrowing or constriction of an artery, leading to reduced blood flow.
examples: Arterioarctia of the coronary artery can cause chest pain.
The scan confirmed arterioarctia in several vessels.
Lifestyle changes may slow the progress of arterioarctia.

senses.definition: Relating to both arteries and capillaries, or to the area where they connect.
examples: The study focused on arteriocapillary changes in the lung.
Arteriocapillary networks supply oxygen to the tissues.
Damage at the arteriocapillary level can affect organ function.

senses.definition: Relating to arteries near the coccyx, the small bone at the bottom of the spine.
examples: The surgeon identified the arteriococcygeal vessels.
Arteriococcygeal branches supply blood to the tailbone area.
Injury to an arteriococcygeal artery can cause local bleeding.

senses.definition: A medical procedure that separates or loosens an artery from surrounding tissue or connection.
examples: Arteriodialysis was performed to free the compressed artery.
The surgeon used arteriodialysis to improve blood flow.
Complications after arteriodialysis are rare.

senses.definition: Abnormal separation or pulling apart of the walls of an artery.
examples: Imaging suggested arteriodiastasis in the carotid artery.
Arteriodiastasis can predispose a vessel to aneurysm.
Trauma is a possible cause of arteriodiastasis.

senses.definition: Thickening and scarring of the wall of an artery due to fibrous tissue.
examples: Long-term inflammation can lead to arteriofibrosis.
Arteriofibrosis was evident in the biopsy sample.
Researchers are exploring treatments to prevent arteriofibrosis.

senses.definition: The growth or formation of new arteries, often to improve blood supply after a blockage.
examples: Exercise may stimulate arteriogenesis in the heart.
Arteriogenesis helps restore blood flow around blocked vessels.
Scientists study arteriogenesis to develop new therapies.

senses.definition: An X-ray picture or image of an artery taken after a special dye is injected.
examples: The arteriogram showed a blockage in the coronary artery.
Her doctor ordered an arteriogram to check blood flow to the leg.
The arteriogram helped guide the surgery.

senses.definition: Plural of arteriogram; several X-ray images of arteries taken after dye injection.
examples: The doctor compared previous arteriograms with the new one.
Arteriograms of the legs revealed several narrow areas.
Modern arteriograms provide very detailed pictures.

senses.definition: A machine or device used to record or measure information about arteries, such as blood pressure or flow.
examples: The technician calibrated the arteriograph before use.
An arteriograph can detect stiffness in large arteries.
The study used an arteriograph to measure arterial function.

senses.definition: Relating to an arteriogram or to the imaging of arteries.
examples: Arteriographic findings confirmed the diagnosis.
The report described arteriographic evidence of blockage.
Arteriographic techniques have improved in recent years.

senses.definition: Plural of arteriography; several medical procedures in which arteries are imaged using dye and X-rays.
examples: The book reviews different arteriographies of the heart.
Modern arteriographies are safer and more accurate.
They compared arteriographies taken over a ten-year period.
